channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TMDA Issues Safety Warning on Vega-100 Male Enhancement Product
item.description: Tanzania Medicines and Medical Devices Authority (TMDA) imeonya wananchi dhidi ya kutumia dawa ya kuongeza uwezo wa kiume inayoitwa Vega-100. Dawa hii inauzwa kama inavyoongeza utendaji wa kiume, laki...

item.title: Knicks take 2-0 lead in Eastern Conference Finals with strong performance from Josh Hart
item.description: The New York Knicks delivered a commanding performance against the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals, winning 109-93 and taking a 2-0 lead in the best-of-seven series. Cle...

item.title: Trump delays AI policy to avoid affecting US competitiveness with China
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced on May 21, 2026, that he would postpone signing an executive order on artificial intelligence. Trump expressed concerns about certain aspects of the order and empha...

item.title: Multiple Ebola Cases Spark Tensions and Border Restrictions in Africa
item.description: On 22 May 2026, eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo witnessed violent protests at Rwampara General Hospital after relatives of a young man who reportedly died from Ebola tried to remove his body ...

item.title: Spring/Summer 2026 Beauty Trends and Shopping Guide
item.description: Metro.Style presents a comprehensive guide to the top beauty products and trends for Spring and Summer 2026. Covering a wide range of items from skincare essentials to cosmetics, this playbook helps r...

item.title: CRTC increases Canadian content fees for streaming platforms
item.description: The Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) has announced new rules requiring streaming platforms such as Netflix, Disney+, and Amazon Prime to contribute significantly more...

item.title: PNP mobilizes tracker teams to serve ICC arrest warrant on Senator Bato dela Rosa
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has deployed tracker teams to locate and arrest Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa following an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court (ICC) in con...

item.title: Femi Otedola shifts $100M from Geregu Power to Dangote Refinery ahead of September IPO
item.description: Nigerian billionaire Femi Otedola don sell him Geregu Power shares sharply to invest huge $100 million inside Dangote Refinery pre-IPO placement. E talk say this IPO fit be generational wealth opportu...

item.title: Former CDC Director Warns of Rapidly Expanding Ebola Outbreak in Africa
item.description: Robert Redfield, former director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), has expressed serious concerns about the current Ebola outbreak affecting the Democratic Republic of the Congo...

item.title: West Bengal retains 500 central forces companies post-Assembly elections to maintain law and order
item.description: The Union Home Ministry has decided to keep 500 companies of Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) deployed in West Bengal till 20th June, following a request from the state government. Each company typ...

item.title: Sonja Henie: From Olympic Skater to Hollywood Star
item.description: Sonja Henie (1912–1969) was a groundbreaking Norwegian figure skater and actress whose influence on both sports and entertainment remains remarkable. Beginning as a child prodigy, Henie dominated wome...

item.title: US House Republicans postpone vote on war powers resolution regarding Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON, USA – Republican leaders in the US House of Representatives decided to postpone a scheduled vote on May 21 regarding a resolution that would require President Donald Trump to seek congress...

item.title: Telcos dispute ACMA’s $7.32 billion spectrum pricing decision
item.description: The Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) has finalised its pricing for expiring spectrum licences, setting a benchmark of $7.32 billion. These licences, used for mobile services and fi...

item.title: APC Officially Picks Jamilu Gwamna as 2027 Gombe Governorship Candidate
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don officially declare Jamilu Gwamna as their governorship candidate for Gombe State ahead of the 2027 elections. Abubakar Danladi, wey be the returning officer for...

item.title: Istanbul Bilgi University Closed After State Seizure Amid Criminal Investigation
item.description: Turkiye’s President Tayyip Erdogan has officially revoked the operational licence of Istanbul Bilgi University, effectively ordering its closure. The university, which was seized by the state last yea...

item.title: Top Selling Books in Canada for the Week of May 23, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail has released its weekly list of bestselling books in Canada for the week of May 23, 2026, based on data provided by BookNet Canada. The list includes rankings across multiple catego...

item.title: Reflections on the Financial and Career Implications of University Education
item.description: Charlotte Ambrose reflects critically on her experience at Durham University and subsequent studies, including a Master's at Bristol University and a journalism qualification. She highlights the finan...

item.title: Teacher calls for Supreme Court review of DepEd’s academic strand changes
item.description: A teacher has formally urged the Supreme Court to temporarily halt and review the Department of Education's recent decision to dissolve certain academic strands in the senior high school curriculum. T...

item.title: Former Football Star Rio Ferdinand to Promote Tanzania Tourism Globally
item.description: Tanzania don appoint Rio Ferdinand, the retired English footballer, as tourism ambassador. Dis wan part of government plan to make Tanzania more popular for travel and wildlife experiences internation...

item.title: How Unequal Bill Splitting Affects Your Finances and Social Etiquette in the UK
item.description: Many Brits frequently overpay on shared bills to avoid awkward conversations about money, a phenomenon described as the 'politeness penalty'. Research by Zopa Bank reveals that one in three people reg...
